heading: Portfolio management framework for National Nuclear Security Administration
label: 6112 Portfolio management framework for National Nuclear Security Administration

children.label: A
children.text: defines the Administration’s portfolio of nuclear weapons stockpile and infrastructure maintenance and modernization programs;

children.label: B
children.text: establishes a portfolio governance structure, including portfolio-level selection criteria, prioritization criteria, and performance metrics;

children.label: C
children.text: outlines the approach of the Administration to managing that portfolio; and

children.label: D
children.text: incorporates the leading practices identified by the Comptroller General of the United States in the report titled “Nuclear Security Enterprise: NNSA Should Use Portfolio Management Leading Practices to Support Modernization Efforts” (GAO-21-398) and dated June 2021; and
complete an integrated, comprehensive assessment of the portfolio management capabilities required to execute the weapons activities portfolio of the Administration.
the progress of the Administrator in developing the framework described in paragraph (1) of subsection (a) and completing the assessment required by paragraph (2) of that subsection; and
the plans of the Administrator for implementing the recommendations of the Comptroller General in the report referred to in paragraph (1)(D) of that subsection.
